frictions: Quiet Cargo suite output only reports aggregate pass counts, so the paired -- --list commands were needed to map assertion requirements to concrete test names.

Quiet Cargo execution (`-q`) suppressed per-test pass output, so the paired `-- --list` commands were required to map assertion IDs to concrete unit tests before using the passing suite runs as execution evidence.

Quiet Cargo output does not identify which assertions are covered unless the matching `-- --list` commands are run first, so test-name mapping had to be established from the list output before using the pass/fail summaries.
Quiet Cargo test output only reported aggregate pass counts, so `-- --list` output was required to map the passing suites back to the contract’s named assertions.
